ages.name: Ancient Age
ages.desc: First fires, first fields, first walls. Everything is undiscovered and every neighbor is a stranger.

ages.name: Classical Age
ages.desc: Philosophy, currency and organized legions. Civilizations begin to see — and covet — one another.

ages.name: Medieval Age
ages.desc: Castles, guilds and crusades. Faith and steel decide the fate of kingdoms.

ages.name: Renaissance Age
ages.desc: Printing, gunpowder and tall ships. The whole globe is suddenly within reach.

ages.name: Industrial Age
ages.desc: Coal smoke over iron rails. Production explodes — and so does the cost of falling behind.

ages.name: Modern Age
ages.desc: Oil, flight and total war. The world shrinks to a chessboard.

ages.name: Information Age
ages.desc: Silicon minds and instant signals. Circuits become as strategic as steel once was.

ages.name: Near Future
ages.desc: Fusion, arcologies and the first steps beyond the cradle. The endgame begins.

deepOcean.name: Deep Ocean
deepOcean.desc: Abyssal open water. Only deep-sea vessels (Compass+) may cross. Cannot be claimed until the Modern era.

ocean.name: Ocean
ocean.desc: Open water over the continental slope. Naval units travel freely; fishing fleets can work it late-game.

coast.name: Coastal Waters
coast.desc: Shallow shelf waters. Rich fisheries and the highway of early trade. Claimable next to coastal cities.

lake.name: Lake
lake.desc: Inland freshwater. Grants +1 Food to adjacent Farms.

iceCap.name: Ice Cap
iceCap.desc: Permanent polar ice. Impassable until Icebreakers (Modern). Research Stations may be built here in the Information era.

tundra.name: Tundra
tundra.desc: Frozen plains of moss and permafrost. Hard to farm, but hides mineral wealth and, later, Oil.

boreal.name: Boreal Forest
boreal.desc: Endless taiga. Superb logging country; slow to develop and cold enough to blunt farming.

grassland.name: Grassland
grassland.desc: Fertile lowlands — the breadbasket biome. Farms here outproduce every other terrain.

plains.name: Plains
plains.desc: Dry open flatland. A balanced all-rounder for food and industry.

forest.name: Temperate Forest
forest.desc: Broadleaf woodland. Lumber Camps thrive; defenders gain cover.

wetland.name: Wetland
wetland.desc: Marsh and bog. Rich in food and unique flora, but most construction requires Drainage (Medieval).

savanna.name: Savanna
savanna.desc: Tropical grassland dotted with acacia. Good herding and caravan country.

steppe.name: Steppe
steppe.desc: Arid shortgrass plain. Horse country — mounted units trained in steppe cities are cheaper.

desert.name: Desert
desert.desc: Sun-blasted waste. Nearly barren — until trade routes, Oil derricks and Solar Farms turn it to gold.

rainforest.name: Rainforest
rainforest.desc: Dense equatorial jungle. Slow going and hard to clear, but a wellspring of medicine and science.

highlands.name: Highlands
highlands.desc: Rolling hills and plateaus. Prime mining land with a natural defensive edge.

mountain.name: Mountain
mountain.desc: High peaks. Impassable until Mountaineering (Renaissance). Observatories and deep mines come later.

volcanic.name: Volcanic
volcanic.desc: Ash-fed soil around active vents. Extremely fertile and mineral-rich — with a chance of eruption events.

food.name: Food
food.desc: Feeds your population. Each population unit eats 1 Food per tick; surplus fills city growth bars, deficit causes starvation (population loss and unrest).

materials.name: Materials
materials.desc: Timber, stone and metal. Spent to construct buildings and train units; the universal industry resource.

gold.name: Gold
gold.desc: The currency. Pays unit and building upkeep, rush-buys production, funds diplomacy and trade deals. A bankrupt nation suffers army desertion.

science.name: Science
science.desc: Research points, generated per tick and invested automatically into the selected technology.

influence.name: Influence
influence.desc: Political reach. Spent to claim tiles, enact policies and make diplomatic proposals. Border tiles cost Influence upkeep that grows with distance from your cities.

coal.name: Coal
coal.desc: Industrial-era strategic resource, extracted by Coal Mines on Coal deposits. Powers Factories and early industrial units. Becomes tradeable on the world market.

oil.name: Oil
oil.desc: Modern-era strategic resource from Oil Wells and Offshore Platforms. Fuels tanks, aircraft and Power Plants. Wars have been started for less.

circuits.name: Circuits
circuits.desc: Advanced manufactured component (not mined!). Chip Fabs convert Materials + Gold into Circuits; fabs need a Rare Earth deposit within your borders. Required by Information/Near-Future buildings and units.

fertile.name: Fertile Soil
fertile.effect: +2 Food to Farms/Plantations on this tile
fertile.desc: Deep loam or volcanic ash soil. The cradle of every great empire.

game.name: Wild Game
game.effect: +2 Food to Hunting Camps; +1 Food unimproved
game.desc: Herds of deer, elk or antelope. Feeds early cities before agriculture scales.

fish.name: Fish Shoals
fish.effect: +2 Food to Fisheries on this tile
fish.desc: Teeming shallows. Coastal nations live and die by them.

stone.name: Stone
stone.effect: +2 Materials to Quarries on this tile
stone.desc: Workable granite, marble or limestone outcrops.

metals.name: Metal Ore
metals.effect: +2 Materials to Mines; required for metal-line units at full strength
metals.desc: Copper, tin and iron veins. Revealed by Bronze Working.

horses.name: Horses
horses.effect: +1 Food, +1 Gold to Pastures; enables cavalry units at -25% cost
horses.desc: Wild herds. Revealed by Animal Husbandry; the engine of ancient conquest.

gems.name: Gems
gems.effect: +3 Gold to Mines on this tile
gems.desc: Precious stones and native gold. Revealed by Currency.

coalDep.name: Coal Deposit
coalDep.desc: Carboniferous seams. Revealed by Steam Power; the fuel of the Industrial age.

oilDep.name: Oil Field
oilDep.desc: Crude reservoirs. Revealed by Combustion. Deserts and shallow seas hide the richest fields.

rareEarth.name: Rare Earths
rareEarth.effect: Required within borders to operate Chip Fabs; +2 Science to Mines here
rareEarth.desc: Neodymium, lithium, cobalt. Revealed by Semiconductors; scarce, strategic, fought over.

techs.name: Hunting
techs.desc: Organized hunts turn wild herds into steady meals.

techs.name: Agriculture
techs.desc: Deliberate sowing — the single greatest gamble in history.

techs.name: Animal Husbandry
techs.desc: Tamed beasts give milk, wool and muscle. Reveals Horses.

techs.name: Fishing
techs.desc: Nets and weirs harvest the shallows.

techs.name: Irrigation
techs.desc: Ditches carry the river to the field.

techs.name: Pottery
techs.desc: Sealed jars defeat rot and rats. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Toolmaking
techs.desc: Flint, bone and antler shaped to purpose.

techs.name: Woodworking
techs.desc: Axes bite; timber becomes wall and hull.

techs.name: Masonry
techs.desc: Dressed stone outlives its maker. +1 city tile.

techs.name: The Wheel
techs.desc: Everything heavy suddenly rolls.

techs.name: Bronze Working
techs.desc: Copper and tin marry into weapons-grade metal. Reveals Metal Ore.

techs.name: Tanning
techs.desc: Hides become armor, straps and tents.

techs.name: Warbands
techs.desc: The first organized violence.

techs.name: Archery
techs.desc: Death at a distance changes every argument.

techs.name: Spearcraft
techs.desc: A wall of points stops a charge cold.

techs.name: Palisades
techs.desc: Sharpened logs buy time when raiders come.

techs.name: Bronze Arms
techs.desc: Bronze blades bite deeper than stone.

techs.name: Raiding
techs.desc: War pays for itself — if you win.

techs.name: Barter
techs.desc: Grain for pots, pots for goats.

techs.name: Weights & Measures
techs.desc: Honest scales make repeat customers.

techs.name: Trade Caravans
techs.desc: Strings of pack animals link distant peoples.

techs.name: Salt Preservation
techs.desc: Salt turns surplus into wealth that keeps.

techs.name: Tribute
techs.desc: Lesser chiefs pay for protection — or else.

techs.name: Dyeworks
techs.desc: Purple cloth is worth its weight in silver.

techs.name: Star Gazing
techs.desc: Patterns in the night sky repay patient watching.

techs.name: Counting
techs.desc: Tallies on bone become arithmetic.

techs.name: Herbalism
techs.desc: Which leaf soothes and which one kills.

techs.name: Writing
techs.desc: Memory outsourced to clay. Everything changes. +1 research option to choose from.

techs.name: Sailing
techs.desc: Wind does the rowing. Land units may embark onto coastal waters.

techs.name: Calendars
techs.desc: Knowing when to plant is half the harvest.

techs.name: Tribal Council
techs.desc: Elders argue so warriors don't have to. Unlocks the first policy slot.

techs.name: Ancestor Worship
techs.desc: The dead advise the living.

techs.name: Customary Law
techs.desc: Precedent settles quarrels before they fester.

techs.name: Chiefdom
techs.desc: One voice speaks for the tribe — and its lands.

techs.name: Ritual Burial
techs.desc: Shared rites bind the community.

techs.name: Warrior Code
techs.desc: Honor makes soldiers hold the line.

techs.name: Fallowing
techs.desc: Rested fields yield double.

techs.name: Aqueducts
techs.desc: Cities drink from mountains a valley away. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Viticulture
techs.desc: Vines turn hillsides into festivals.

techs.name: Beekeeping
techs.desc: Sweetness, wax and better harvests.

techs.name: Fish Salting
techs.desc: Fleets can now feed inland cities.

techs.name: Terrace Farming
techs.desc: Steps carved into hills. Farms may be built on Highlands.

techs.name: Iron Working
techs.desc: Iron is everywhere once you know how to ask.

techs.name: Carpentry
techs.desc: Joinery raises roofs faster and truer.

techs.name: Concrete
techs.desc: Liquid stone poured into any shape.

techs.name: Glassblowing
techs.desc: Sand becomes windows, vessels and lenses.

techs.name: Millstones
techs.desc: Grinding grain at scale frees a hundred hands.

techs.name: Road Building
techs.desc: All roads lead somewhere profitable.

techs.name: Formation Tactics
techs.desc: The phalanx is greater than the sum of its spears.

techs.name: Swordsmanship
techs.desc: A sidearm becomes a profession.

techs.name: Horsemanship
techs.desc: Cavalry arrives before the news of it does.

techs.name: Siegecraft
techs.desc: Walls stop being an answer.

techs.name: Naval Rams
techs.desc: Warships built to sink, not just carry.

techs.name: Fortifications
techs.desc: Stone curtains around everything you love.

techs.name: Currency
techs.desc: Stamped metal that everyone trusts. Reveals Gems.

techs.name: Moneylending
techs.desc: Money today for more money tomorrow.

techs.name: Port Trade
techs.desc: Harbors where cargo and rumor change hands.

techs.name: Taxation
techs.desc: The one certainty besides death.

techs.name: Caravanserai
techs.desc: Safe lodging doubles the caravan routes.

techs.name: Luxury Trade
techs.desc: Silk and spice buy prestige abroad.

techs.name: Philosophy
techs.desc: Asking why, professionally.

techs.name: Mathematics
techs.desc: The universe turns out to be written in it.

techs.name: Astronomy
techs.desc: Stars become a map for ships and seasons. Ships may brave the open Ocean — islanders can finally reach their neighbors.

techs.name: Classical Medicine
techs.desc: Observation replaces exorcism, mostly.

techs.name: Literature
techs.desc: Epics carry your name past every border.

techs.name: Engineering
techs.desc: Cranes, levers and arrogance.

techs.name: City States
techs.desc: The city becomes an idea worth dying for. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Monarchy
techs.desc: One crown to answer every question.

techs.name: State Religion
techs.desc: The gods take a side — yours.

techs.name: Citizenship
techs.desc: Belonging becomes a legal status worth having.

techs.name: Drama & Games
techs.desc: Bread is only half of it.

techs.name: Republic
techs.desc: Power on a rotating schedule.

techs.name: Heavy Plough
techs.desc: Iron shares turn heavy northern clay to bread.

techs.name: Crop Rotation
techs.desc: Three fields, endless cycle, no famine.

techs.name: Horse Collar
techs.desc: A padded strap doubles every draft animal.

techs.name: Drainage
techs.desc: Ditches and dikes. Buildings may be constructed on Wetlands.

techs.name: Windmills
techs.desc: The sky grinds your grain for free.

techs.name: Fishponds
techs.desc: Fresh fish on demand, even far from the sea.

techs.name: Metal Casting
techs.desc: Molten metal poured into moulds — bells and cannon alike.

techs.name: Sawmilling
techs.desc: Water-driven blades never tire.

techs.name: Masons' Guilds
techs.desc: Craft secrets, apprentices and cathedral-grade skill.

techs.name: Shipwrights
techs.desc: Purpose-built yards launch bigger hulls.

techs.name: Charcoal Burning
techs.desc: Forests condensed into furnace fuel.

techs.name: Clockwork
techs.desc: Gears that measure time itself.

techs.name: Feudal Levies
techs.desc: Land for service — an army you don't pay in coin.

techs.name: Chivalry
techs.desc: Armored aristocracy on horseback.

techs.name: Crossbows
techs.desc: A week of training defeats a lifetime of armor.

techs.name: Castles
techs.desc: A stone answer to every question of ownership. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Siege Engines
techs.desc: Counterweights hurl the sky at walls.

techs.name: War Cogs
techs.desc: High-sided merchantmen turned floating forts.

techs.name: Guilds
techs.desc: Monopoly, quality control and mutual aid in one charter. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Letters of Credit
techs.desc: Paper promises move fortunes past bandits.

techs.name: Trade Fairs
techs.desc: A season of commerce in a fortnight.

techs.name: Silk Road
techs.desc: The desert becomes a highway of silk and spice.

techs.name: Merchant Leagues
techs.desc: Port cities band together and set the prices.

techs.name: Mint Standards
techs.desc: Sound coinage everyone can trust.

techs.name: Monasticism
techs.desc: Quiet halls copy the wisdom of ages.

techs.name: Universities
techs.desc: Degrees, debates and dangerous ideas.

techs.name: Algebra
techs.desc: Unknowns get names and surrender.

techs.name: Optic Lenses
techs.desc: Ground glass extends the eye. +1 vision range.

techs.name: The Compass
techs.desc: A needle that always knows north. Ships may enter Ocean tiles.

techs.name: Papermaking
techs.desc: Knowledge gets cheap enough to spread.

techs.name: Feudal Contract
techs.desc: Obligations all the way up and down.

techs.name: Common Law
techs.desc: Same rules in every shire.

techs.name: Royal Courts
techs.desc: Splendor is statecraft.

techs.name: Pilgrimage Routes
techs.desc: Faith travels, and spends.

techs.name: Heraldry
techs.desc: Banners tell soldiers what they fight for.

techs.name: Great Charter
techs.desc: Even the crown signs contracts now.

techs.name: New World Crops
techs.desc: Potatoes and maize rewrite the food math.

techs.name: Enclosures
techs.desc: Hedged fields, higher yields, displaced peasants.

techs.name: Selective Breeding
techs.desc: Livestock engineered by patience.

techs.name: Plantations
techs.desc: Cash crops at colonial scale.

techs.name: Botany
techs.desc: Plants catalogued, crossed and conquered.

techs.name: Cash Crops
techs.desc: Sugar, tobacco, cotton — sweet, addictive profit.

techs.name: Printing Press
techs.desc: Arguments at industrial volume.

techs.name: Manufactories
techs.desc: Many hands under one roof, one process. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Advanced Metallurgy
techs.desc: Blast furnaces and boring machines.

techs.name: Ship Framing
techs.desc: Skeleton-first construction launches leviathans.

techs.name: Instrument Making
techs.desc: Precision tools for precision thoughts.

techs.name: Water Mills
techs.desc: Rivers put to work around the clock.

techs.name: Gunpowder
techs.desc: Chemistry ends the age of armor.

techs.name: Cannonry
techs.desc: Castles become quaint overnight.

techs.name: Bastion Forts
techs.desc: Star-shaped geometry against the cannonball.

techs.name: Cavalry Doctrine
techs.desc: Pistol, sabre and shock.

techs.name: Naval Gunnery
techs.desc: The broadside is diplomacy's exclamation mark.

techs.name: Professional Army
techs.desc: Drilled, salaried, always ready.

techs.name: Banking
techs.desc: Money that earns money while you sleep.

techs.name: Joint-Stock Companies
techs.desc: Risk chopped into shares and sold.

techs.name: Insurance
techs.desc: Catastrophe priced by actuaries.

techs.name: Customs & Excise
techs.desc: Every border crossing pays its toll.

techs.name: Chartered Companies
techs.desc: Private empires with public flags.

techs.name: Colonial Trade
techs.desc: Triangle routes and treasure fleets.

techs.name: Scientific Method
techs.desc: Test it or toss it. +1 research option to choose from.

techs.name: Heliocentrism
techs.desc: The Earth is demoted; science is promoted.

techs.name: Anatomy
techs.desc: Medicine finally looks inside.

techs.name: Cartography
techs.desc: Charts tame the abyss. Ships may cross Deep Ocean.

techs.name: Mechanics
techs.desc: Force, mass and motion get equations.

techs.name: Early Chemistry
techs.desc: Alchemy sobers up.

techs.name: Renaissance Art
techs.desc: Beauty as a state asset.

techs.name: Embassies
techs.desc: Permanent ears in every court.

techs.name: Absolutism
techs.desc: L'état, c'est moi.

techs.name: Humanism
techs.desc: Man becomes the measure.

techs.name: Religious Tolerance
techs.desc: Heretics pay taxes too.

techs.name: Civil Service
techs.desc: Exams, files and a state that remembers. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Seed Drills
techs.desc: Rows, not scatter. Yields jump.

techs.name: Fertilizers
techs.desc: Guano, nitrates and the end of fallow fields.

techs.name: Canning
techs.desc: Armies march on tinned rations.

techs.name: Refrigeration
techs.desc: Distance stops spoiling dinner.

techs.name: Steam Trawlers
techs.desc: Engines drag the nets now.

techs.name: Veterinary Science
techs.desc: Herds get doctors of their own.

techs.name: Steam Power
techs.desc: Boiling water moves the world. Reveals Coal.

techs.name: Factory System
techs.desc: Steam-driven mass production. Consumes Coal.

techs.name: Steelmaking
techs.desc: Bessemer's converter makes steel cheap as iron.

techs.name: Railways
techs.desc: The countryside shrinks to a timetable. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Machine Tools
techs.desc: Machines that make machines.

techs.name: Telegraphy
techs.desc: News outruns the horse at last.

techs.name: Rifling
techs.desc: Spin stabilization makes every soldier a marksman.

techs.name: Modern Artillery
techs.desc: Indirect fire from beyond the horizon.

techs.name: Ironclads
techs.desc: Wooden navies are obsolete by lunchtime.

techs.name: Gatling Guns
techs.desc: Arithmetic applied to infantry charges.

techs.name: Military Science
techs.desc: Staff colleges study war like a subject.

techs.name: Logistics
techs.desc: Amateurs discuss tactics; professionals discuss supply.

techs.name: Stock Exchanges
techs.desc: Capital finds ambition in seconds.

techs.name: Industrial Banking
techs.desc: Banks big enough to fund railroads.

techs.name: Department Stores
techs.desc: Everything under one gaslit roof.

techs.name: Free Trade
techs.desc: Tariffs fall; hulls fill.

techs.name: Corporations
techs.desc: Immortal persons made of paper.

techs.name: Advertising
techs.desc: Manufacturing desire itself.

techs.name: Public Education
techs.desc: Literacy becomes infrastructure.

techs.name: Germ Theory
techs.desc: The invisible enemy finally has a name.

techs.name: Electricity
techs.desc: Lightning in a copper leash.

techs.name: Thermodynamics
techs.desc: You can't win, you can't break even.

techs.name: Natural History
techs.desc: Deep time and descent with modification.

techs.name: Sanitation
techs.desc: Cities stop poisoning themselves. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Nationalism
techs.desc: The tribe, industrialized.

techs.name: Labor Movements
techs.desc: Eight hours' work deserves eight hours' rest.

techs.name: Constitutionalism
techs.desc: The rules above the rulers.

techs.name: Emancipation
techs.desc: Liberty extends its franchise.

techs.name: Mass Journalism
techs.desc: The fourth estate opens for business.

techs.name: Imperial Doctrine
techs.desc: Maps colored with entitlement.

techs.name: Mechanized Farming
techs.desc: One tractor replaces forty field hands.

techs.name: Synthetic Fertilizer
techs.desc: Bread pulled from thin air (and natural gas).

techs.name: Pesticides
techs.desc: Chemistry versus the locust.

techs.name: Industrial Fishing
techs.desc: Factory ships that vacuum the sea.

techs.name: Crop Hybrids
techs.desc: The Green Revolution begins.

techs.name: Food Processing
techs.desc: Shelf-stable everything.

techs.name: Combustion Engines
techs.desc: Portable power in a steel block. Reveals Oil.

techs.name: Assembly Lines
techs.desc: The product moves; the worker doesn't.

techs.name: Plastics
techs.desc: Any shape, any color, forever.

techs.name: Oil Refining
techs.desc: Crude cracked into fuel, tar and money.

techs.name: Electric Grid
techs.desc: Power on tap in every district. Consumes Coal. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Offshore Drilling
techs.desc: Chasing oil beneath the waves.

techs.name: Modern Infantry
techs.desc: Squad weapons, radios and doctrine.

techs.name: Armored Warfare
techs.desc: Cavalry reborn in welded steel. Consumes Oil.

techs.name: Military Aviation
techs.desc: The high ground rises to 10,000 meters.

techs.name: Strategic Bombing
techs.desc: War arrives over the factory district.

techs.name: Submarine Warfare
techs.desc: Terror by periscope.

techs.name: Modern Navy
techs.desc: Fast, gray and bristling.

techs.name: Consumer Economy
techs.desc: Prosperity measured in shopping bags.

techs.name: Central Banking
techs.desc: A thermostat for the whole economy.

techs.name: International Trade
techs.desc: Containers standardize the planet.

techs.name: Tourism
techs.desc: Your landmarks become industries.

techs.name: Consumer Credit
techs.desc: Buy now, pay later, forever.

techs.name: Petrochemical Markets
techs.desc: Black gold gets a futures desk.

techs.name: Radio
techs.desc: One voice in a million living rooms.

techs.name: Atomic Theory
techs.desc: Matter's smallest pieces, biggest implications.

techs.name: Antibiotics
techs.desc: Infection loses its death sentence.

techs.name: Industrial R&D
techs.desc: Invention becomes a department.

techs.name: Rocketry
techs.desc: Pointed at the sky, for now.

techs.name: Public Health
techs.desc: Clean water saves more lives than any drug. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Universal Suffrage
techs.desc: Everyone gets a lever.

techs.name: Propaganda
techs.desc: Morale, manufactured.

techs.name: Welfare State
techs.desc: The state catches those who fall.

techs.name: Internationalism
techs.desc: Leagues, nations, united.

techs.name: Civil Defense
techs.desc: Sirens, shelters and drills.

techs.name: Total Mobilization
techs.desc: The whole economy reports for duty.

techs.name: Precision Agriculture
techs.desc: Satellites plan every furrow.

techs.name: GMO Crops
techs.desc: The genome becomes farmland.

techs.name: Aquaculture
techs.desc: Fish farmed like wheat.

techs.name: Global Cold Chain
techs.desc: Strawberries in winter, everywhere.

techs.name: Desert Irrigation
techs.desc: Drip lines green the dunes. Farms may be built on Desert.

techs.name: Sustainable Farming
techs.desc: Farming that plans past the quarter.

techs.name: Semiconductors
techs.desc: Sand that thinks. Reveals Rare Earths; Chip Fabs produce Circuits.

techs.name: Robotics
techs.desc: Tireless arms on the factory floor.

techs.name: Composite Materials
techs.desc: Lighter than aluminum, stronger than steel.

techs.name: Recycling Systems
techs.desc: Yesterday's scrap is tomorrow's stock.

techs.name: Renewable Energy
techs.desc: Power without smoke.

techs.name: Industrial Automation
techs.desc: Lights-out manufacturing.

techs.name: Guided Missiles
techs.desc: Ordnance with a mailing address.

techs.name: Jet Aircraft
techs.desc: The sound barrier surrenders.

techs.name: Mechanized Infantry
techs.desc: Riflemen at highway speed.

techs.name: Composite Armor
techs.desc: Layered ceramics shrug off shells. Consumes Oil + Circuits.

techs.name: Carrier Groups
techs.desc: A mobile piece of national territory.

techs.name: Drone Reconnaissance
techs.desc: Eyes that never blink or sleep.

techs.name: Electronic Banking
techs.desc: Money becomes weightless.

techs.name: Globalization
techs.desc: One planet, one supply chain.

techs.name: E-Commerce
techs.desc: The whole bazaar in your pocket.

techs.name: Venture Capital
techs.desc: Betting big on clever people.

techs.name: Commercial Aviation
techs.desc: Anywhere on Earth by dinnertime.

techs.name: Service Economy
techs.desc: Wealth from work you can't drop on your foot.

techs.name: Computers
techs.desc: Arithmetic at the speed of light. +1 research option to choose from. +1 city tile.

techs.name: The Internet
techs.desc: Everyone talks to everyone, about everything.

techs.name: Satellites
techs.desc: The whole globe on one screen. Reveals the world map.

techs.name: Polar Science
techs.desc: Icebreakers open the last frontier. Units may cross Ice Caps.

techs.name: Genetics
techs.desc: The instruction manual for life, annotated.

techs.name: Academic Networks
techs.desc: Universities merge into research ecosystems.

techs.name: Mass Media
techs.desc: The narrative becomes the territory.

techs.name: International Law
techs.desc: Treaties with teeth, occasionally.

techs.name: Environmentalism
techs.desc: The planet gets a seat at the table.

techs.name: Digital Governance
techs.desc: Bureaucracy at broadband speed. +1 city tile.

techs.name: Soft Power
techs.desc: Culture conquers where armies can't.

techs.name: Open Society
techs.desc: Freedom turns out to be productive.

techs.name: Vertical Farming
techs.desc: Forty stories of lettuce downtown.

techs.name: Synthetic Proteins
techs.desc: Steak without the steer.

techs.name: Arctic Agriculture
techs.desc: Engineered crops shrug off the frost. Farms may be built on Tundra.

techs.name: Ocean Farming
techs.desc: Kelp forests planted like orchards.

techs.name: Soil Engineering
techs.desc: Dead dirt brought back to life.

techs.name: Closed-Loop Food Systems
techs.desc: Nothing wasted, ever.

techs.name: Fusion Power
techs.desc: A star in a bottle. Consumes Circuits.

techs.name: Nanomaterials
techs.desc: Matter engineered atom by atom.

techs.name: Robotic Factories
techs.desc: Factories that never open the lights.

techs.name: Deep Crust Mining
techs.desc: Boreholes to the mantle's doorstep.

techs.name: Seasteading
techs.desc: Cities that float. Build on Coastal Waters.

techs.name: Smart Grids
techs.desc: Every watt routed by algorithm.

techs.name: Power Armor
techs.desc: One soldier, one walking fortress.

techs.name: Autonomous Weapons
techs.desc: The operator is now optional.

techs.name: Railguns
techs.desc: Mach 7 answers to strategic questions.

techs.name: Aegis Systems
techs.desc: A fleet under one shield.

techs.name: Battle Mechs
techs.desc: Armor learns to walk.

techs.name: Orbital Recon
techs.desc: Nothing on the surface hides.

techs.name: Crypto Finance
techs.desc: Trust, decentralized and volatile.

techs.name: Fusion Economy
techs.desc: Energy too cheap to argue about.

techs.name: Space Tourism
techs.desc: The view is worth the ticket.

techs.name: Automated Logistics
techs.desc: Cargo that routes itself.

techs.name: Post-Scarcity Markets
techs.desc: Economics after abundance.

techs.name: Orbital Manufacturing
techs.desc: Zero-g foundries, flawless crystals.

techs.name: Quantum Computing
techs.desc: Every answer at once, briefly.

techs.name: Artificial Intelligence
techs.desc: The tool that improves itself.

techs.name: Life Extension
techs.desc: Aging, negotiated downward.

techs.name: Climate Engineering
techs.desc: The thermostat of last resort.

techs.name: Space Elevator
techs.desc: A ribbon to orbit; the price of space drops to freight.

techs.name: Unified Physics
techs.desc: One equation to bind them all.

techs.name: Global Governance
techs.desc: Humanity's HOA.

techs.name: Digital Democracy
techs.desc: The agora, redistributed.

techs.name: Universal Basic Income
techs.desc: A floor under every citizen.

techs.name: Arcologies
techs.desc: A city in a single building. +2 city tiles.

techs.name: Planetary Union
techs.desc: Borders begin to feel old-fashioned.

techs.name: Ascension Program
techs.desc: Begin the Starlight Ark project — the path to Science Victory.

buildings.name: City Center
buildings.desc: The beating heart of a settlement. Founded by Settlers; produces a little of everything and claims the surrounding tiles.

buildings.name: Hunting Camp
buildings.desc: Smokehouses and snare lines. Best where Wild Game roams.

buildings.name: Farm
buildings.desc: Tilled fields. The foundation of every empire — but only where soil and rain allow.

buildings.name: Pasture
buildings.desc: Herds on the hoof. Steppe nomads swear by it; a Horses deposit makes it strategic.

buildings.name: Fishery
buildings.desc: Boats, nets and drying racks on a water tile. Fish Shoals double the catch.

buildings.name: Quarry
buildings.desc: Cut stone for walls and wonders. Stone deposits boost it further.

buildings.name: Lumber Camp
buildings.desc: Axes and log flumes. Boreal forests are the best timberland on the planet.

buildings.name: Granary
buildings.desc: Sealed storage against rot and raiders. +2 max population.

buildings.name: Shrine
buildings.desc: A sacred site binding the people to their land and leaders.

buildings.name: Barracks
buildings.desc: Drill yards and armories. Land units trained in this city start with +25% experience and cost 10% less.

buildings.name: Mine
buildings.desc: Shafts chasing veins into the dark. Requires rugged terrain; Metal Ore, Gems and Rare Earths all boost it.

buildings.name: Aqueduct
buildings.desc: Fresh water for fountains and fields. +3 max population.

buildings.name: Workshop
buildings.desc: Artisans under one roof turning raw stock into finished goods.

buildings.name: City Walls
buildings.desc: Stone fortifications. +15 city defense.

buildings.name: Market
buildings.desc: Stalls, scales and haggling. The tax base begins here.

buildings.name: Library
buildings.desc: Scrolls and scholars. Knowledge compounds like interest.

buildings.name: Temple
buildings.desc: Marble devotion. Faith legitimizes borders like nothing else.

buildings.name: Amphitheater
buildings.desc: Spectacle keeps citizens loyal and visitors spending.

buildings.name: Port
buildings.desc: Wharves and warehouses on a coastal water tile. Naval units may be trained by this city.

buildings.name: Windmill
buildings.desc: Grinds grain wherever the wind blows steady.

buildings.name: Sawmill
buildings.desc: Water-powered blades. Upgrade over the lumber camp.

buildings.name: Forge
buildings.desc: Bellows and anvils. Metal Ore within your borders makes it +25% productive.

buildings.name: Castle
buildings.desc: Feudal power in stone. +25 city defense and a statement to the neighbors.

buildings.name: Guild Hall
buildings.desc: Charters, monopolies and member dues.

buildings.name: Monastery
buildings.desc: Scriptoria in quiet places. Thrives in remote terrain.

buildings.name: University
buildings.desc: Lecture halls and rivalries. The engine of the late game starts here.

buildings.name: Harbor
buildings.desc: Deep moorings and cranes. Upgrade over the port for oceangoing trade.

buildings.name: Trade Post
buildings.desc: A caravanserai on the long routes. Deserts turn from obstacle to asset.

buildings.name: Plantation
buildings.desc: Cash crops in ordered rows. Fertile Soil boosts it; rainforests suit it best.

buildings.name: Printing Works
buildings.desc: Movable type spreads your ideas — and your propaganda.

buildings.name: Manufactory
buildings.desc: Pre-industrial mass production under one roof.

buildings.name: Observatory
buildings.desc: Clear high-altitude skies. Mountains become temples of science.

buildings.name: Shipyard
buildings.desc: Slipways for warships. Naval units from this city are 20% cheaper.

buildings.name: Fortress
buildings.desc: Star-fort geometry. +40 city defense.

buildings.name: Bank
buildings.desc: Vaults, ledgers and compound interest.

buildings.name: Customs House
buildings.desc: Every crossing pays. Coastal cities profit most.

buildings.name: Theater
buildings.desc: Culture with box-office receipts.

buildings.name: Coal Mine
buildings.desc: Shafts into the carboniferous. Requires a Coal deposit; feeds factories and fleets.

buildings.name: Factory
buildings.desc: Steam-driven mass production. Consumes 1 Coal per tick — idle without it.

buildings.name: Steelworks
buildings.desc: Bessemer converters. Consumes 1 Coal; Metal Ore within borders boosts output 25%.

buildings.name: Rail Depot
buildings.desc: A junction of iron roads. Units trained in this city move 15% faster.

buildings.name: Stock Exchange
buildings.desc: Fortunes made and unmade by the closing bell.

buildings.name: Public School
buildings.desc: Universal literacy: the greatest research multiplier ever built.

buildings.name: Hospital
buildings.desc: Wards and antiseptics. +4 max population and faster army healing nearby.

buildings.name: Military Academy
buildings.desc: Officers with diplomas. Units from this city start with +50% experience.

buildings.name: Sewers
buildings.desc: Unglamorous, undefeated. +4 max population.

buildings.name: Telegraph Office
buildings.desc: Orders and news at lightspeed (minus the last mile).

buildings.name: Oil Well
buildings.desc: A derrick nodding over an Oil Field. The strategic resource of the century.

buildings.name: Offshore Platform
buildings.desc: Drilling through the seabed. Requires an offshore Oil Field.

buildings.name: Power Plant
buildings.desc: Coal-fired electricity. Consumes 1 Coal; every district hums.

buildings.name: Oil Refinery
buildings.desc: Crude in, money out. Consumes 1 Oil per tick.

buildings.name: Research Laboratory
buildings.desc: White coats and patent filings.

buildings.name: Broadcast Tower
buildings.desc: Your anthem on every frequency.

buildings.name: Airfield
buildings.desc: Runways and hangars. Required to train air units; extends unit vision +2 around it.

buildings.name: Mechanized Farm
buildings.desc: Tractors and combine harvesters. Consumes 1 Oil per tick.

buildings.name: Shopping Mall
buildings.desc: Climate-controlled commerce.

buildings.name: Water Treatment Plant
buildings.desc: Clean water at municipal scale. +5 max population.

buildings.name: Chip Fabricator
buildings.desc: Cleanrooms etching silicon. Consumes 4 Materials + 2 Gold per tick and requires a Rare Earth deposit inside your borders. The only source of Circuits.

buildings.name: Data Center
buildings.desc: Racks of humming knowledge. Cold climates halve the cooling bill (+25% in Tundra/Boreal).

buildings.name: Tech Campus
buildings.desc: Where startups and seminars share a parking lot.

buildings.name: Solar Farm
buildings.desc: Mirrors drinking sunlight. Deserts become power-houses.

buildings.name: Wind Farm
buildings.desc: Turbines on ridge and shore.

buildings.name: Recycling Plant
buildings.desc: The landfill becomes a mine.

buildings.name: Media Network
buildings.desc: Every screen in the world is your embassy.

buildings.name: Polar Research Station
buildings.desc: Science at the end of the world — the only building possible on Ice Caps.

buildings.name: International Airport
buildings.desc: Hub status: achieved. +1 trade route capacity for your nation.

buildings.name: Satellite Uplink
buildings.desc: Dishes tracking a private constellation.

buildings.name: Trawler Dock
buildings.desc: Industrial aquaculture pens. Fish Shoals double the harvest.

buildings.name: Fusion Plant
buildings.desc: A tokamak sunrise. Consumes 2 Circuits per tick and outproduces every other power source.

buildings.name: Vertical Farm
buildings.desc: Crops stacked to the sky. Works in any climate — the end of famine.

buildings.name: Robotic Factory
buildings.desc: Zero workers, zero breaks. Consumes 2 Circuits per tick.

buildings.name: Quantum Lab
buildings.desc: Refrigerators colder than deep space, thoughts faster than light.

buildings.name: Deep Crust Mine
buildings.desc: Kilometers down, past every exhausted seam. Any deposit on this tile yields double.

buildings.name: Seastead Platform
buildings.desc: A floating district anchored offshore. The sea becomes suburbia.

buildings.name: Climate Controller
buildings.desc: Weather by committee. Shields your nation from climate-type random events.

buildings.name: Arcology
buildings.desc: A vertical city of a million souls. +10 max population.

buildings.name: Orbital Elevator
buildings.desc: The stairway to the stars — and a prerequisite of the Starlight Ark. One per nation; must stand within 15° of the equator.

units.name: Settler
units.desc: Founds a new city (consumed). The most important unit in the game — escort it.
units.notes: Founds city on any land tile that allows cities, 4+ tiles from another city.

units.name: Scout
units.desc: Fast eyes for the early game. Ignores terrain movement penalties.

units.name: Archer
units.notes: Ranged: strikes adjacent tiles without taking counter-damage.

units.name: Galley
units.desc: Coast-hugging oars. Cannot enter open Ocean until Astronomy.

units.name: Horseman
units.desc: Fast flanks and faster retreats. Requires Horses.

units.name: Trireme
units.desc: A bronze beak at ramming speed.

units.name: Pikeman
units.desc: A hedge of steel. +50% vs cavalry.

units.name: Knight
units.desc: Shock aristocracy. Requires Horses and Metal Ore.

units.name: Musketeer
units.desc: Pike and shot; the end of the knight.

units.name: Caravel
units.desc: The explorer's ship — crosses deep ocean.

units.name: Ironclad
units.desc: Steam and armor plate. Consumes Coal to build.

units.name: Fighter
units.desc: Air superiority. Requires an Airfield in the training city.
units.notes: Flies over any terrain. Cannot capture tiles.

units.name: Bomber
units.notes: Flies. 4x vs cities. Cannot capture tiles.

units.name: Destroyer
units.desc: Fast escort, sub-hunter, jack of all trades.

units.name: Aircraft Carrier
units.desc: A floating airbase and the center of any fleet.

units.name: Recon Drone
units.desc: Unblinking eyes, no crew to lose.
units.notes: Flies. Huge sight radius. Cannot capture tiles.

units.name: Exo-Infantry
units.desc: Powered armor squads that shrug off small arms.

units.name: Drone Swarm
units.desc: A thousand small decisions, all hostile.

units.name: Railgun Artillery
units.desc: Hypervelocity slugs; the horizon is a suggestion.

units.name: Aegis Cruiser
units.desc: A shield over the whole fleet.

units.name: Battle Mech
units.desc: The queen of the late-game battlefield.

policies.name: Agrarian Focus
policies.desc: Every hand to the harvest. The scribes can wait.

policies.name: Artisan Patronage
policies.desc: State silver flows to the workshops.

policies.name: Open Markets
policies.desc: Low walls and open gates make rich cities — and tempting ones.

policies.name: State Granaries
policies.desc: The crown feeds the people; the people multiply.

policies.name: Mercantilism
policies.desc: Hoard bullion, tax rivals, apologize never.

policies.name: Free Enterprise
policies.desc: The invisible hand works overtime.

policies.name: Planned Economy
policies.desc: Five-year plans and quota boards.

policies.name: Export Economy
policies.desc: Everything is for sale, in bulk, FOB.

policies.name: Green Transition
policies.desc: Prosperity that doesn't eat its seed corn.

policies.name: Innovation Economy
policies.desc: Subsidize the future; expense the past.

policies.name: Warrior Culture
policies.desc: Songs of battle drown out quieter pursuits.

policies.name: Border Watch
policies.desc: Beacons on every hill, eyes on every pass.

policies.name: Conscription
policies.desc: Every citizen owes a season under arms.

policies.name: Mercenary Contracts
policies.desc: Loyalty rented by the campaign.

policies.name: Standing Army
policies.desc: Barracks never empty, borders never bare.

policies.name: Naval Tradition
policies.desc: The sea is a province like any other — ours.

policies.name: Fortress Doctrine
policies.desc: Why march when you can make them come to you?

policies.name: Total War Doctrine
policies.desc: The entire nation is the war effort.

policies.name: Deterrence
policies.desc: Peace through visible, expensive readiness.

policies.name: Rapid Deployment
policies.desc: First to arrive writes the terms.

policies.name: Council of Elders
policies.desc: Wisdom over vigor; consensus over conquest.

policies.name: Temple Economy
policies.desc: The granary, the mint and the altar are one institution.

policies.name: Scholar Patronage
policies.desc: Genius on retainer.

policies.name: Guild Charters
policies.desc: Quality guaranteed; competition, less so.

policies.name: Civic Festivals
policies.desc: Bread, circuses and belonging.

policies.name: Enlightenment Salons
policies.desc: Dangerous ideas served with tea.

policies.name: Public Welfare
policies.desc: No citizen left to the wolves.

policies.name: National Mythos
policies.desc: A story big enough to die for.

policies.name: Open Society
policies.desc: Talent immigrates; ideas circulate.

policies.name: Digital Citizenship
policies.desc: The state as a service, citizens as users.

perks.name: Children of the Soil
perks.desc: Your farmers coax bread from stone. +15% Food, forever.

perks.name: Forge Masters
perks.desc: Craft runs in the blood. +15% Materials.

perks.name: Merchant Princes
perks.desc: Everything has a price; you set it. +15% Gold.

perks.name: Philosopher Kings
perks.desc: Rulers who read. +15% Science.

perks.name: Born Diplomats
perks.desc: Words win what wars would waste. +15% Influence.

perks.name: Expansionists
perks.desc: The horizon is a to-do list. -20% tile claim cost.

perks.name: Frontier Spirit
perks.desc: Distance means nothing to your settlers. -25% border upkeep.

perks.name: Great Builders
perks.desc: Monuments rise in months, not decades. -15% construction cost.

perks.name: Warrior Blood
perks.desc: Courage is a family heirloom. +10% attack.

perks.name: Stalwart Defenders
perks.desc: Your land fights beside you. +15% defense.

perks.name: Seafarers
perks.desc: Salt in the veins. +15% naval attack, +10% movement.

perks.name: Prolific People
perks.desc: Cradles outnumber coffins. +20% population growth.

perks.name: Drilled Legions
perks.desc: An army production line. -15% unit cost.

perks.name: Quartermasters
perks.desc: Fed, shod and paid on time. -20% unit upkeep.

perks.name: Silver Tongues
perks.desc: Trust arrives before your envoys do. +10% Influence, +1 trade route.

perks.name: Pathfinders
perks.desc: No blank spaces on your maps. +1 vision, +10% movement.

perks.name: Resilient Stock
perks.desc: Hard to kill, quick to mend. +50% healing, luckier events.

perks.name: Golden Hoard
perks.desc: Old money, carefully kept. +10% Gold, -10% building upkeep.

perks.name: Industrialists
perks.desc: Smoke means progress. +10% Materials, +25% Factory output.

perks.name: Petroleum Barons
perks.desc: You can smell a reservoir from horseback. +50% Oil Well output.

perks.name: Silicon Pioneers
perks.desc: The fab is the new cathedral. +50% Chip Fab output, +5% Science.

perks.name: Memetic Culture
perks.desc: Your jokes conquer continents. +20% Influence.

perks.name: War Economy
perks.desc: Factories that pivot to arsenals overnight.

perks.name: Terraformers
perks.desc: No biome is beyond the plough. +10% Food, +25% Farm output.

specializations.name: Breadbasket
specializations.desc: This city feeds the nation. +30% Food, -10% Science here.

specializations.name: Forge City
specializations.desc: Anvils before altars. +30% Materials, -10% Food here.

specializations.name: Trade Nexus
specializations.desc: All roads, one toll booth. +30% Gold, -10% defense here.

specializations.name: Scholar's Haven
specializations.desc: Rents are high, ideas are higher. +30% Science, -10% Gold here.

specializations.name: Holy City
specializations.desc: Pilgrims arrive; legitimacy radiates. +35% Influence, -10% Materials here.

specializations.name: Bastion
specializations.desc: A city built like an argument-ender. +50% city defense, -10% Gold here.

specializations.name: Naval Base
specializations.desc: Slipways and shore leave. Naval units -25% cost, +10% Gold here. Requires a Port.

specializations.name: Garrison City
specializations.desc: Every block is a barracks. Units trained here cost -20%.

specializations.name: Cultural Jewel
specializations.desc: The city other nations write poems about. +20% Influence, +15% Gold here.

specializations.name: Tech Hub
specializations.desc: Garages that eat industries. +25% Science, +15% Gold here.

events.name: Bumper Harvest
events.desc: Perfect rains and a gentle autumn. The granaries overflow.

events.name: Drought
events.desc: The rains fail. Rivers shrink to memory. -25% Food for 18 ticks.

events.name: Gold Vein Struck
events.desc: A miner's pick rings against something soft and yellow.

events.name: Trade Boom
events.desc: Foreign markets can't get enough of your goods. +30% Gold for 20 ticks.

events.name: Recession
events.desc: Credit tightens; ledgers bleed red. -30% Gold for 20 ticks.

events.name: Currency Debasement
events.desc: Someone has been shaving the coins. Confidence — and treasury — shrink.

events.name: Wealthy Caravan
events.desc: A great caravan chooses your roads and pays handsomely for the privilege.

events.name: General Strike
events.desc: Tools down until demands are met. -30% Materials for 15 ticks.

events.name: Oil Glut
events.desc: Every well overperforms at once. Tanks brim; prices wobble.

events.name: Chip Shortage
events.desc: One flooded fab on the far side of the world, and everything stalls. -50% Chip Fab output.

events.name: Earthquake
events.desc: The ground forgets its manners. A building is reduced to rubble.

events.name: Volcanic Eruption
events.desc: Fire from below. Destruction now — but the ash will feed a generation. +15% Food afterward.

events.name: Wildfire
events.desc: A wall of flame through the timberlands. -20% Materials for 12 ticks.

events.name: Great Flood
events.desc: The river reclaims its plain and everything built on it.

events.name: Harsh Winter
events.desc: Snow to the rooftops. Armies freeze in place; stores dwindle.

events.name: Plague
events.desc: It arrives with the traders and stays for years. Cities empty.

events.name: Pandemic
events.desc: Airports spread it faster than rats ever could.

events.name: Locust Swarm
events.desc: The sky darkens and descends on the fields.

events.name: Storm at Sea
events.desc: A once-a-century tempest. Fleets and embarked armies take 25% damage.

events.name: Solar Flare
events.desc: The sun sneezes; the grid staggers. -25% Science for 10 ticks.

events.name: Golden Age
events.desc: Everything works at once. Historians will argue why for centuries. +15% to everything.

events.name: Grand Festival
events.desc: Feasting, games and marriages. The nation feels like one family.

events.name: Religious Revival
events.desc: Fervor sweeps the land. +30% Influence for 20 ticks.

events.name: Civil Unrest
events.desc: Grievances boil over into the streets.

events.name: Spy Scandal
events.desc: Your ambassador's letters, published. Everyone is Shocked.

events.name: Refugee Wave
events.desc: War or famine abroad drives thousands to your borders.

events.name: A Great Thinker
events.desc: Once in a generation, a mind that moves the whole species forward.

events.name: Cultural Icon
events.desc: A poet, a painter, a voice — suddenly your nation is fashionable.

events.name: Border Dispute
events.desc: Farmers, then soldiers, argue over a boundary stone.

events.name: Dynastic Marriage
events.desc: Two houses joined; a border quietly relaxed.

events.name: Barbarian Raid
events.desc: Riders on the horizon, smoke behind them. Raiders spawn near your border.

events.name: Pirate Fleet
events.desc: Your sea lanes fly someone else's flag today.

events.name: Veteran Cadre
events.desc: Old soldiers step forward to train the new. +20% combat strength for 20 ticks.

events.name: Desertions
events.desc: Pay is late, boots are thin, home is far. Armies lose 15% strength.

events.name: War Bonds
events.desc: The public buys victory on installment.

events.name: Peace Movement
events.desc: Crowds fill the squares demanding the boys come home.

events.name: Prospectors' Find
events.desc: Surveyors return breathless: there is wealth under your soil. A new deposit appears in your territory.

events.name: Deposit Exhausted
events.desc: The seam pinches out. One of your deposits is gone for good.

events.name: Ancient Ruins
events.desc: Under the hill: an older city than yours, and its treasury.

events.name: Foreign Blueprints
events.desc: A defector arrives with a briefcase. You don't ask questions.

domination.name: Domination Victory
domination.desc: Capture or control every rival capital. A nation whose capital falls survives, diminished — but a nation that holds them all rules everything that matters.
domination.check: Own all original capital cities (yours included).

science.name: Science Victory
science.desc: Research the Ascension Program (Near Future Statecraft), build an Orbital Elevator, then fund the five-stage Starlight Ark project (each stage costs 2,000 Science + 1,500 Materials + 50 Circuits, paid from stockpiles). Launch the Ark to win.
science.check: Complete 5 Starlight Ark stages after building the Orbital Elevator.

economic.name: Economic Victory
economic.desc: Accumulate 75,000 Gold in your treasury while maintaining at least 5 active trade agreements. Money talks — eventually it gives orders.
economic.check: Treasury ≥ 75,000 Gold and ≥ 5 active trade deals.

hegemony.name: Hegemony Victory
hegemony.desc: Control 40% of the world's land tiles. When the map is mostly your color, the argument is over.

score.name: Score Victory
score.desc: If no one has won by year 4000 (the calendar is uniform: 1 tick = 1 month, 12 months per year, in every era), the nation with the highest score takes the crown of history. Score = population x3 + tiles x1 + techs x5 + buildings x2 + gold/100.

changelog.title: Monthly ticks
changes: THE CALENDAR NOW TICKS IN MONTHS: one tick = one month, 12 ticks = one year. The tick rate is 12× faster (0.6 s per tick at 1×) so a game-year still takes the same 7.2 s of real time — the overall pace is unchanged, but the world moves in finer steps.

All per-tick flows rescaled: economy (food, gold, materials, science, influence, strategic resources) applies 1/12 of its per-year rate each month. Data files and the wiki still list per-year figures.

Durations rebalanced to sensible month counts: buildings take time×1.5 months (a 6-time farm ≈ 9 months), unit training 4–36 months by cost, units move and fight at one-third strength per monthly round, healing 0.8 hp/month, desertion and war-weariness decay scaled to monthly rates.
Diplomacy on the new clock: trade deals run 240 months (20 years) paying monthly, opinions drift once a year, proposal cooldowns are 120 months after an accept / 240 after a decline.
Events roll monthly (same yearly frequency as before) and their year-based durations convert to months on application.
HUD date line shows the month ("Ancient • Year 51, M4"); saves store the month counter (older saves derive it from the year).
Self-test long-run extended to 1,500 monthly ticks (~125 years) with calendar and research-progress checks.

changelog.title: Consent diplomacy, living fog, island freedom
changes: DIPLOMACY: nothing friendly is automatic anymore. AI offers of alliance, non-aggression, trade and peace arrive as PROPOSALS you accept or decline (war still arrives uninvited). New actions: Send Gift (+relations for Gold), Demand Tribute (extort weaker rivals), Denounce (−25 relations, +15 Influence) and Break Pact.

FOG OF WAR is no longer permanent: you see only around your territory (+1), city tiles (+3 — cities watch farther), units (their sight; scouts farther) and allied vision. Leave, and the map re-fogs. Satellites remains the one source of permanent (dimmed) global terrain intel.

ISLAND FREEDOM: open ocean now has two gameplay tiers — mid-depth seas are crossable once Classical-era Astronomy is researched, so island starts can reach their neighbors five eras earlier; only the abyssal deep still waits for Renaissance Cartography.

INFLUENCE: claiming territory now costs exponentially more with distance from your cities (×1.4 per ring), and holding it drains Influence every tick, also exponential (×1.25 per ring) — far-flung land is a continuous commitment, not a one-time purchase.
Tiles show their yields on the map: floating per-tile production labels (e.g. "2F 1M") appear over your land when zoomed in — no clicking required.
Pace slowed 3×: one tick every 7.2 s at 1× speed.
The mouse wheel now always scrolls UI lists when the cursor is over a panel — the camera only zooms over open map.
Self-test grown to 39 checks (fog transience, ocean tiers, exponential costs, proposals, gifts, pacts).

changelog.title: Cities vs. territory, annexation, per-tile production
changes: Buildings can now only be built on tiles that are PART of a city. A new city is just its center tile; adjacent owned territory must be ANNEXED into the city (costs Materials + Gold + Influence scaling with city size) before it can be developed.

City size is research-gated: 15 urban technologies across the ages (Masonry, Pottery, Aqueducts, City States, Guilds, Castles, Civil Service, Manufactories, Sanitation, Railways, Electric Grid, Public Health, Computers, Digital Governance, Arcologies) each grant +1 city tile (+2 for Arcologies), growing the per-city cap from 1 to ~17.

Plain territory only trickles a small share of its biome yields; claiming it costs sharply more Influence the farther it lies from your cities (distance factor raised ~40%). Territory decays first when Influence runs dry — city tiles are protected.

Cities are now visually distinct from territory: bright inner rings around each city's tile group, whitened urban tint on city tiles (strongest on the center), faint wash on territory. Captured cities transfer all their district tiles.
Every owned tile now reports what it actually produced last tick — in the tile panel ("Producing: +2.4 Food · +1.0 Mat") and in the hover readout.

UI click reliability: panels no longer rebuild under the cursor (this was eating clicks), and click-vs-drag detection measures distance from the press point instead of accumulated jitter (10 px threshold).
AI annexes high-value tiles into its cities and builds only on city tiles; save format bumped to v3 (city tile lists).

changelog.title: TileGame2: slower pace, uniform eras
changes: The game is now called TileGame2 (launcher renamed to TileGame2.bat).
Pacing slowed: one simulation tick every 2.4 seconds at 1× (was 1.2 s) — use 2×/4× when you want history to hurry.

The calendar no longer imitates real-world history: it starts at Year 1 and advances a uniform 1 year per tick in every era (no more era-dependent tick timing). Eras remain research-gated at 20 techs each, so every era is roughly the same length.
Age data dropped its historical start-years; the Score victory deadline is now year 4000 on the uniform calendar.

changelog.title: The old world returns + research draws
changes: WORLD GENERATION replaced with a faithful port of the original TileGame project's generator: 1024×512 equirect grid, domain-warped tectonic plates, ridged elevation, ITCZ/Hadley moisture with tectonic rain shadows, 25 tile types (jungles, mangroves, coral reefs, salt flats, alpine meadows…), lake filling/smoothing, tiny-island culling, coastal shallow strips, and meandering rivers routed over depression-filled elevation. The globe uses the original palette with per-pixel field re-classification — the old game's exact look.

Gameplay layers were kept: district tiles now derive their 8 slots from the composition of the ported grid beneath them; old tile types map onto the existing biome data (rivers count as fertile floodplains and boost settlement odds).

RESEARCH is now a weighted DRAW: pick from a hand of 3 options (Writing, Scientific Method and Computers each add +1). Weights lean toward the branches you are spec'd into — researched history, policies, perks and city specializations all pull their branch, and current-age techs appear more often. Reroll the hand for Influence (cost scales with age). The tech tree screen highlights the current hand in gold; AIs pick from their own draws by personality.

UI: new research chooser cards with branch colors; image placeholder system across the whole interface (top-bar resources, tech cards, biome headers, buildings, events, perks, city portrait, menu logo) — drop PNGs into game/assets/icons/ (see its README) and they replace the placeholders automatically.
Menu: ocean-coverage slider now drives the generator's sea level; terrain detail option removed (the shader is resolution-independent).
Self-test grown to 26 checks (research draw, affinity weighting, reroll costs).

changelog.title: Districts, per-pixel terrain & the planet look
changes: DISTRICT SYSTEM: every hex tile now has 8 building slots, apportioned from the biome composition the tile spans (largest-remainder method) — a tile that is 70% forest / 20% ocean / 10% desert gets 5 forest, 2 ocean and 1 desert slot. Each slot type allows different buildings and applies its own biome output multiplier, so one tile can host a lumber camp, a fishery and a farm side by side (Stellaris-style).

City centers occupy one slot of their tile; deposit extractors are limited to one per deposit; a tile-level deposit bonus goes to the first matching building; buildings can be demolished from the new slot grid UI in the tile panel.
Tile panel shows district composition ("3× Plains · 3× Grassland · 2× Highlands") and a clickable 8-slot grid with per-slot build menus; AI evaluates (tile, slot, building) triples.

PER-PIXEL TERRAIN: biome classification and shading moved into a fragment shader fed by smooth interpolated climate fields — coastlines, lakes and biome borders are now crisp at any zoom, with hypsometric altitude tinting, per-fragment relief noise and moisture pocketing. Default render mesh raised to 12× tile resolution (~576k vertices; 6×/9×/12×/15× menu options).

THE PLANET LOOK: unexplored fog now reads as a darkened planet surface instead of a black void; added an additive fresnel atmosphere rim, a 1,100-star field, filmic tonemapping with subtle glow, and specular sun glints on water and sea ice.
Overlay, border, grid and outline layers now hug the terrain relief per-tile instead of floating at fixed radii; camera drag is 1:1 grab-the-globe at any zoom.

Fixed the inside-out globe rendering (Godot expects clockwise front faces) that could make the planet look see-through; fixed a barycentric indexing crash at simulation-grid boundaries that caused a black screen on world generation.
Save format bumped to version 2 (per-slot buildings); older saves are ignored by Continue.
Self-test suite extended to 21 checks including district slot apportionment and multi-building tiles.

changelog.title: High-resolution terrain
changes: Render mesh resolution decoupled from the climate simulation: terrain now renders at 6× / 9× / 12× the tile frequency (up to ~830,000 vertices on a large Ultra world) while plates/climate simulate at 3× and gameplay tiles stay unchanged.
New arithmetic-dedup geodesic builder (corner/edge/interior index math, no hash maps) keeps massive render grids fast to construct.

Climate fields (elevation, temperature, moisture, volcanism, lakes, land proximity) are barycentrically interpolated from the simulation grid onto every render vertex, then biomes are re-classified per vertex — coastlines and biome boundaries are now pixel-crisp instead of tile-blocky.
Extra render-scale relief noise adds coastline wiggle and ridge texture; shorelines get sandy beach blending; terrain speckle replaced with smooth noise grain.
World generation is multithreaded (WorkerThreadPool) across all CPU cores.
New "Terrain Detail" option on the main menu (Standard 6× / High 9× / Ultra 12×); the menu shows progress while forging the world.
Fixed a face-winding bug that made the globe render inside-out (Godot expects clockwise front faces), which could make the planet look see-through.

changelog.title: Godot engine port + high-resolution terrain
changes: Ported the entire game from the browser prototype to Godot 4.4 (native Windows app; run AEONS.bat or open game/ in Godot).

Terrain generation and rendering now use a high-resolution geodesic field at 3x the tile frequency — coastlines, mountain ranges and climate zones are fully independent of the gameplay tile grid, which samples the field by majority vote.
Terrain mesh renders with true vertex relief and accumulated smooth normals; mountains rise from the silhouette.
Tectonic plates now use domain-warped weighted Voronoi assignment (faster and cleaner boundaries at high resolution); mid-ocean ridges kept submarine.
Per-tile fog/political overlay layer with translucent nation tints; border ribbons; selection/hover outlines; 3D city labels, unit tokens with hp pips, and deposit markers.

Full Godot UI: resource top bar with live rates, tile panel (claim / build with placement-rule feedback), city panel (training, specializations), scrollable 8-age tech tree, policies screen, diplomacy screen, event and perk dialogs, victory screen, pause menu, main menu with seed/size/nations/ocean/climate/difficulty.
Integration self-test suite (19 checks: founding, claiming, building, training, combat, fog, trade, policies, save/load, 400-tick stability) — run with AEONS_TEST=1.
Save/load to Godot user:// storage; Continue button on the main menu.
The browser version remains in the repository as a prototype; data/*.js stays the single authoring source, exported to game/data/gamedata.json for the engine and read directly by reference.html.

changelog.title: Initial release
changes: Seeded parametric world generation: tectonic plates with motion vectors, convergent/divergent boundary stress (mountain belts, island arcs, rifts, trenches), hotspot island chains.

Climate model: latitude temperature bands with altitude lapse, prevailing-wind moisture advection (trade winds / westerlies / polar easterlies), orographic rain shadows, ITCZ equatorial rains and horse-latitude deserts.
18 biomes classified from temperature x moisture x elevation, including inland seas, lakes, volcanic soil and polar ice.
Goldberg-polyhedron globe (12 pentagons + hexagons; 2,562 / 4,002 / 5,762 tile world sizes).
8 ages from Ancient to Near Future; age advancement via researched-tech count with a perk pick (1 of 3) at each new age.
288 technologies across 6 branches (Agriculture, Industry, Military, Commerce, Science, Statecraft).
76 buildings with biome placement rules, biome output multipliers, deposit requirements and strategic-resource consumption.
42 units from Warrior to Battle Mech across melee/ranged/cavalry/siege/naval/air classes.
Resources: Food, Materials, Gold, Science, Influence + strategic Coal, Oil and manufactured Circuits.
10 tile deposit types with tech-gated reveals (Metal Ore, Coal, Oil, Rare Earths...).
Influence-based border expansion: claim cost and upkeep scale with distance from cities; capital projects influence 40% further; borders decay when influence runs dry.
Three-state fog of war: unknown / discovered (terrain intel only, dimmed) / visible (full detail from owned tiles +2, units and allies).
30 policies in limited slots, 24 nation perks, 10 city specializations.
40 random events with era scaling, conditions, durations and choice events.
Diplomacy: war/peace, alliances, non-aggression pacts, trade deals, relations with opinion drift, AI proposals.
AI nations with expansion, construction, research, diplomacy and war behaviors across 3 difficulty levels.
5 victory conditions: Domination, Science (Starlight Ark), Economic, Hegemony, Score (year 2200).
RTS controls: drag-rotate globe, wheel zoom, WASD/QE, click select, right-click orders, pause/1x/2x/4x speed.
Save/load to browser storage. Map lenses: terrain, political, biome, elevation, temperature, moisture, plates.
